categories.description: Fly reels by weight class, arbor, and drag.

lineWeightSystem.primaryUse: Small streams, spooky trout, light dries
Small streams and creeks, panfish, delicate dry fly
Small to medium trout streams, dry fly and light nymphing
Larger trout, smallmouth bass, wind, light streamers
Bass, steelhead, light saltwater, larger streamers
Saltwater (bonefish, redfish), salmon, pike, big bass

lineTapers.name: Weight-Forward
lineTapers.note: Most popular; easy distance and turnover.

lineTapers.name: Shooting Taper / Head
lineTapers.note: Short head for max distance with running line.

lineTapers.name: Triangle Taper
lineTapers.note: Continuous front taper for smooth energy transfer.

notes: Rod, line, and reel weights should match (e.g. a 5wt rod takes a 5wt line and a 5/6 reel).
Felt-soled wading boots are restricted or banned in several jurisdictions to limit aquatic invasive species — check local regulation.
Tippet breaking strengths are nominal and vary by manufacturer and material.

gear.name: 3-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 3wt single-hand fly rod. Trout, panfish on small water.

gear.name: 4-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 4wt single-hand fly rod. Small to medium trout streams.

gear.name: 5-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 5wt single-hand fly rod. All-around trout — the default workhorse.

gear.name: 6-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 6wt single-hand fly rod. Bigger trout, bass, wind, light streamers.
useCases: Bigger trout, bass, wind, light streamers

gear.name: 7-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 7wt single-hand fly rod. Bass, steelhead, light salt, big streamers.
useCases: Bass, steelhead, light salt, big streamers

gear.name: 8-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 8wt single-hand fly rod. Saltwater flats, salmon, pike.

gear.name: 9-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 9wt single-hand fly rod. Stripers, permit, salmon, big predators.

gear.name: 10-Weight Fly Rod
gear.description: 10wt single-hand fly rod. Heavy saltwater, musky.

gear.name: Euro Nymphing Rod (3wt)
gear.description: Long, soft-tipped rod built for tight-line (euro) nymphing and sighter leaders.

gear.name: Fiberglass Fly Rod (5wt)
gear.description: Slow, full-flexing glass rod for close-quarters small-stream fishing.

gear.name: Tenkara Rod
gear.description: Fixed-line telescopic Japanese rod fished without a reel; line attaches to the tip.

gear.name: Switch Rod (6wt)
gear.description: Hybrid rod castable single- or two-handed for swinging flies and light Spey.

gear.name: Spey Rod (7wt)
gear.description: Two-handed rod for Spey casting large water for salmon and steelhead.

gear.name: Saltwater Fly Rod (9wt)
gear.description: Fast, powerful saltwater rod with sealed components for the flats and surf.

gear.name: Fly Reel 3/4
gear.description: Light trout reels; click drag for small fish.
useCases: Light trout reels; click drag for small fish

gear.name: Fly Reel 5/6
gear.description: All-around trout reel; sealed disc drag.
useCases: All-around trout reel; sealed disc drag

gear.name: Fly Reel 7/8
gear.description: Bass, steelhead, light salt.

gear.name: Fly Reel 9/10
gear.description: Saltwater reel with strong sealed drag.
useCases: Saltwater reel with strong sealed drag

gear.name: Fly Reel 11/12
gear.description: Big-game saltwater reel, high backing capacity.
useCases: Big-game saltwater reel, high backing capacity

gear.name: Classic Click-Pawl Reel
gear.description: Traditional narrow-arbor reel; palming drag and audible click.

gear.name: WF Floating Trout Line
gear.description: Weight-forward floating line — the default for dry-fly and nymph trout fishing.

gear.name: DT Floating Line
gear.description: Double-taper floater for delicate presentation and roll casting; reversible to extend life.

gear.name: WF Intermediate Line
gear.description: Slow-sinking clear/camo line that fishes just under the surface film.

gear.name: WF Sink-Tip Line
gear.description: Floating body with a sinking tip to get streamers down while keeping mending control.

gear.name: Full-Sinking Line — Type 3
gear.description: Uniform-sinking line for fishing deep in lakes and reservoirs.

gear.name: Bass / Bug Taper Line
gear.description: Short powerful front taper to turn over bulky poppers and bass bugs.

gear.name: Saltwater WF Floating Line
gear.description: Tropical-spec floating line with a stiff core that resists heat and turns over big flies.

gear.name: Skagit Head
gear.description: Short, heavy shooting head for two-hand casting heavy sink-tips and big flies.

gear.name: Scandi Head
gear.description: Longer, finer shooting head for lighter flies and delicate Spey presentation.

gear.name: Euro Nymphing Line
gear.description: Thin level line used with long mono leaders for tight-line nymphing.

gear.name: Dacron Backing 20 lb
gear.description: Standard 20 lb braided dacron backing for trout and light reels.

gear.name: Dacron Backing 30 lb
gear.description: Heavier 30 lb dacron for saltwater and big-game reels.

gear.name: Gel-Spun Backing 50 lb
gear.description: Thin, strong gel-spun backing that maximises capacity on saltwater reels.

gear.name: 7.5' 3X Tapered Leader
gear.description: Short leader for streamers and windy days.
useCases: Short leader for streamers and windy days

gear.name: 9' 4X Tapered Leader
gear.description: The all-purpose trout leader.

gear.name: 9' 5X Tapered Leader
gear.description: Low-vis subsurface leader for nymphs.

gear.name: 12' 6X Tapered Leader
gear.description: Long leader for spooky fish and flat water.
useCases: Long leader for spooky fish and flat water

gear.name: Furled Leader
gear.description: Hand-furled leader that turns over softly and lasts many sessions; add tippet to the end.

gear.name: Poly-Leader Sink-Tip
gear.description: Looped sinking poly-leader that converts a floating line into a sink-tip.

gear.name: Saltwater Tapered Leader
gear.description: Heavy fluorocarbon tapered leader for flats species and abrasion resistance.

gear.name: Adams
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating general mayfly / mixed hatch.

gear.name: Parachute Adams
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating mayfly dun, high visibility.

gear.name: Elk Hair Caddis
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating adult caddisfly.

gear.name: Royal Wulff
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating attractor / high-floating searching fly.

gear.name: Blue-Winged Olive (BWO)
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating Baetis mayfly dun.

gear.name: Pale Morning Dun (PMD)
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating Ephemerella mayfly dun.

gear.name: Griffith's Gnat
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating midge cluster.

gear.name: Stimulator
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating stonefly / large caddis / hopper.

gear.name: Comparadun
gear.description: Dry pattern imitating mayfly dun (low-riding, no hackle).

gear.name: RS2
gear.description: Emerger pattern imitating mayfly/midge emerger.

gear.name: Barr's BWO Emerger
gear.description: Emerger pattern imitating Baetis emerging nymph.

gear.name: Partridge & Orange Soft Hackle
gear.description: Wet pattern imitating emerging caddis / drowned insect.

gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating slim mayfly nymph.

gear.name: Gold-Ribbed Hare's Ear
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating general buggy nymph.

gear.name: Copper John
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating weighted attractor nymph.

gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating midge larva/pupa.

gear.name: Prince Nymph
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating stonefly / general attractor.

gear.name: Frenchie
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating euro-style hot-spot mayfly nymph.

gear.name: Perdigon
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating fast-sinking euro nymph.

gear.name: San Juan Worm

gear.name: Stonefly Nymph (Pat's Rubber Legs)
gear.description: Nymph pattern imitating stonefly nymph.

gear.name: Clouser Minnow
gear.description: Streamer pattern imitating baitfish.

gear.name: Muddler Minnow
gear.description: Streamer pattern imitating sculpin / baitfish.

gear.name: Sculpin / Sculpzilla
gear.description: Streamer pattern imitating bottom-dwelling sculpin.

gear.name: Zonker
gear.description: Streamer pattern imitating baitfish with rabbit-strip action.

gear.name: Chernobyl Ant
gear.description: Terrestrial pattern imitating large foam terrestrial / stonefly.

gear.name: Dave's Hopper

gear.name: Foam Beetle
gear.description: Terrestrial pattern imitating beetle.

gear.name: Flying Ant
gear.description: Terrestrial pattern imitating ant (winged).

gear.name: Crazy Charlie
gear.description: Saltwater pattern imitating shrimp.

gear.name: Gotcha
gear.description: Saltwater pattern imitating shrimp / small baitfish.

gear.name: Lefty's Deceiver
gear.description: Saltwater pattern imitating baitfish.

gear.name: Merkin Crab
gear.description: Saltwater pattern imitating crab.

gear.name: Surf Candy
gear.description: Saltwater pattern imitating slim baitfish (epoxy).

gear.name: Breathable Chest Waders
gear.description: Stockingfoot breathable chest waders — the year-round default for deep water.

gear.name: Breathable Waist Waders
gear.description: Cooler, lower-profile waist-high waders for warm weather and shallow wading.

gear.name: Neoprene Chest Waders
gear.description: Insulated neoprene waders for very cold water; warm but less breathable.

gear.name: Hip Waders
gear.description: Thigh-high boots for shallow creeks and bank fishing.

gear.name: Rubber-Sole Wading Boots
gear.description: Vibram rubber-soled boots — travel-friendly and legal in felt-restricted areas.

gear.name: Felt-Sole Wading Boots
gear.description: Felt soles grip slick rock well but are restricted in some jurisdictions.

gear.name: Studded Rubber Boots
gear.description: Carbide studs added to rubber soles for maximum traction on slime and snow.

gear.name: Wet-Wading Sandal/Boot
gear.description: Quick-draining footwear for wet wading in warm weather without waders.

gear.name: Sling Pack
gear.description: Single-strap pack that swings to the front for access without removal.

gear.name: Chest Pack
gear.description: Compact front-worn pack for essentials when travelling light.

gear.name: Hip Pack
gear.description: Lumbar pack that keeps weight off the shoulders and rotates to the front.

gear.name: Fishing Vest
gear.description: Classic multi-pocket vest organising tippet, boxes, and tools across the torso.

gear.name: Fishing Backpack
gear.description: Larger pack for long hikes, overnight trips, and extra layers.

gear.name: Fly Fishing Lanyard
gear.description: Neck lanyard holding a few core tools for the most minimalist setup.

gear.name: Rubber-Mesh Catch-and-Release Net
gear.description: Soft rubber mesh protects fish slime and doesn't tangle flies — best for release.

gear.name: Wooden Landing Net
gear.description: Classic teardrop wooden net; heirloom aesthetic with a fish-friendly bag.

gear.name: Long-Handle Boat Net
gear.description: Extended-handle net for reaching fish from a drift boat or raft.

gear.name: Nippers
gear.description: Sharp clippers for trimming tippet and tag ends; many include a hook-eye pin.

gear.name: Forceps / Hemostats
gear.description: Locking forceps to remove hooks safely and pinch down barbs.

gear.name: Hook Hone
gear.description: Pocket stone to restore hook points dulled on rocks and teeth.

gear.name: Knot Tying Tool
gear.description: Multi-tool that speeds nail knots and clears head cement from hook eyes.

specs.function: tie nail/clinch knots, clear hook eyes

gear.name: Fly-Tying Scissors
gear.description: Fine-tipped scissors for trimming materials and precise streamside cuts.

gear.name: Zinger / Retractor
gear.description: Spring-loaded retractor that keeps nippers and forceps tethered and handy.

gear.name: Floatant (Gel)
gear.description: Silicone gel applied to dry flies to keep them riding high.

gear.name: Desiccant Powder
gear.description: Drying powder that revives a waterlogged or fish-slimed dry fly.

gear.name: Strike Indicators
gear.description: Surface indicators that signal a nymph take and set fishing depth.

gear.name: Split Shot
gear.description: Crimp-on weights (lead-free preferred) to get nymphs into the strike zone.

gear.name: Tippet Rings
gear.description: Tiny rings that join leader and tippet so you stop shortening the leader.

specs.function: connect leader to tippet, save leader

gear.name: Fly Box
gear.description: Foam or compartment box to organise and protect flies by type.

gear.name: Line Cleaner / Dressing
gear.description: Dressing that cleans grime off fly line and restores slick shooting.

gear.name: Leader Straightener
gear.description: Rubber pads that straighten coiled leaders for cleaner turnover.

gear.name: UV-Cure Resin
gear.description: Light-cured resin for building durable fly heads and field repairs.

gear.name: Polarized Sunglasses
gear.description: Polarized lenses cut surface glare to spot fish and protect the eyes.

gear.name: Headlamp
gear.description: Hands-free light for tying on flies at dawn, dusk, and the hike out.

gear.name: Stream Thermometer
gear.description: Water thermometer to time hatches and avoid stressing fish in warm water.

gear.name: Sun Hoody
gear.description: Lightweight UPF hoody for all-day sun protection on the water.

gear.name: Neck Gaiter / Buff
gear.description: Sun-protective neck gaiter for face and neck coverage.

gear.name: Wading Jacket
gear.description: Short-cut waterproof-breathable jacket designed to sit above the waterline.

gear.name: Sun Gloves
gear.description: Fingerless UPF gloves protecting the backs of the hands while keeping dexterity.

gear.name: Wide-Brim / Cap
gear.description: Brimmed hat with a dark underbrim to cut glare and shade the face.
